top5.reason: Thailand's LTR Retirement Visa provides a 10-year renewable stay; Bumrungrad and Samitivej hospitals handle complex chronic conditions at ~30% of US costs, and the $3,000 couple budget covers premium health insurance plus a comfortable lifestyle.

Malaysia's MM2H programme offers a renewable long-stay visa; Gleneagles and Pantai hospitals deliver bilingual English-Malay specialist care; $3,000/month is generous for a couple in Penang or Kuala Lumpur.

Lake Chapala and Ajijic host 15,000+ US retirees with English-speaking cardiologists and $150/month chronic-disease clinics; warm climate satisfies the climate dealbreaker and $3,000 is very comfortable for two.

Medellín's spring-like climate at 1,500 m eliminates extreme-heat concerns; English-speaking specialists and cardiac care cost ~20% of US prices, and a retired couple lives comfortably on $3,000 in El Poblado.

Da Nang and Hội An have established US-expat communities and improving private-hospital infrastructure; $3,000 provides a generous coastal lifestyle, though specialist care is shallower than Thailand.
